text.title: requirements-validation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for requirements-validation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stakeholder signs off without having read the requirements document.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rubber-stamp approval creates a false baseline; defects discovered during design or testing are traced back to an approved requirement, making them expensive change requests rather than cheap pre-baseline fixes.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates sign-off form with "Approved by: Product Owner" when no human has approved.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hallucinated approvals create false baselines; any resolved/approved state must link to a verifiable evidence anchor (chat permalink, ticket transition, signed PDF hash).

failure-mode.description: Agent asked "are these requirements OK?" trends toward yes (confirmation-bias scoring).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs are politeness-biased; use adversarial framing — "find at least 3 ways each requirement could be misinterpreted" — before asking it to grade.

failure-mode.description: Agent quotes spec.md back to itself as evidence_quote instead of citing the elicitation source.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Self-referential evidence cannot detect requirements that accurately restate an incorrect elicitation; evidence must come from the source document under review, not the artifact being validated.

failure-mode.description: Agent proposes new requirements during the validation session ("while we're here, we should also...").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validation modifies existing requirements; it does not extend scope. Extensions return to elicitation so they can be properly captured, analyzed, and prioritized before being baselined.

failure-mode.description: Agent runs tests and labels the result "validation".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validation = are we building the right thing (pre-development); verification = are we building it right (post-implementation). These are separate gates with different artifacts and authorities.
